phases.label: operator bundle materialization
phases.detail: This measured phase comes from the exact trusted-LAN launcher and covers topology-contract generation, failure-drill generation, workflow freeze, and bring-up fixture capture in one local operator bundle.

phases.label: mac coordinator bring-up validation

worker_timings.detail: Measured from the retained Mac MLX bring-up report. This is a real same-node contributor/validator gate on the Mac host.

phases.detail: The retained Mac bring-up report digest `b7f20eca5c4aa228e927d62c9544ac0dcc8a31777d66395c5e26a19bfbd48db5` stays bound to the exact swarm topology and provides one measured MLX Metal preflight for the rehearsal.

phases.label: linux contributor bring-up validation

worker_timings.detail: Measured from the retained Linux RTX 4080 swarm bring-up report. This remains a retained inventory plus same-node harness measurement, not a live remote node session.

phases.detail: The retained Linux bring-up report digest `4a74e2ca035dc0cf802d0866b89e889ae384f18e8608dbd9b8c7954a6d036dd9` proves the same-node CUDA harness and explicit contract posture, but it is not yet a remote two-node measurement.

phases.label: contributor execution projection

worker_timings.detail: Projected from the current same-node MLX contributor gate. The Mac contributor finishes first and then waits on the slower Linux contributor in the current two-node plan.
Projected from the current same-node CUDA contributor harness and the first trusted-LAN role split. This is the slower contributor in the rehearsal model.

phases.detail: This phase is simulated. The repo does not yet retain a live two-node contributor execution receipt for the exact lane, so the report keeps the backend split explicit instead of pretending the execution timing is already measured.

phases.label: artifact upload and staging projection

worker_timings.detail: The Mac upload path can complete earlier, but the validator still waits for the slower Linux upload before it can score a comparable contribution set.
The Linux upload path is projected as the slower staging path in the current rehearsal model and therefore dictates validator start time.

phases.detail: This phase is simulated from the exact workflow plan, expected upload digests, and current failure drills. The lane still lacks live two-node upload receipts for the trusted-LAN run.

phases.label: validator and aggregation projection

worker_timings.detail: The first lane keeps validator and aggregation work on the Mac coordinator. That remains one serial coordinator phase in the rehearsal and one real blocker before a faster live lane exists.

phases.detail: Validator and aggregation timing remain simulated because the exact lane still lacks a live two-node contribution set to score and merge.

bottlenecks.detail: The measured local rehearsal still spends most of its wallclock in serial operator bundle materialization and cargo-driven contract generation.
bottlenecks.remediation: Keep this as an operator rehearsal cost only. Do not mistake it for live contributor runtime, and avoid calling the lane low-friction until the bundle path stops dominating iteration time.

bottlenecks.detail: The current backend-specific execution projection leaves the Mac contributor idle for 1900 ms while the Linux contributor finishes.
bottlenecks.remediation: Either reduce Linux-side work per window or accept that the first live attempt is a bottleneck-mapping exercise rather than a speedup claim.

bottlenecks.detail: Validator and aggregation work remain single-host coordinator phases on the Mac node.
bottlenecks.remediation: Do not claim parallel end-to-end training throughput until validator and aggregation timing are measured against a real two-node contribution set.

remaining_blockers: The exact first swarm lane still lacks a live two-node contributor execution receipt across the trusted-LAN topology.
Upload, validator, and aggregation timings are still simulated because no live comparable contribution set has been retained yet.
The Linux node still depends on a retained RTX 4080 inventory report rather than a live remote machine probe owned by the trusted-LAN launcher.
No promotion or explicit no-promotion receipt exists yet for the exact two-node lane.
recommendation_reason: Do not describe the first live attempt as ready yet. The exact trusted-LAN topology, launch bundle, and failure drills are now frozen, but the actual two-node contributor, upload, validator, and aggregation path is still not retained as live machine-legible evidence.
claim_boundary: This report is a rehearsal-grade bottleneck report for the exact first swarm trusted-LAN lane. It combines measured local operator-bundle and bring-up timing with explicit simulated contributor, upload, validator, and aggregation timing. It is not a live two-node swarm evidence bundle.
